summary: Broaden decisionConsistency pattern detection: multi-signal regexes, dynamic session selection, extracted helper method
context: decisionConsistency scored 57-64 across all conditions with high variance (stdev 12-18). Coordination tools didn't improve it — full-twining (58.4) scored lower than baseline (64.2). Diagnosis showed: (1) Sessions 2 and 6 consistently failed across ALL conditions because the single event-driven regex missed valid implementations, (2) hardcoded session indices [1,5,7] and [8-11] skipped sessions that touched notification code, (3) binary match/no-match gave no partial credit.
rationale: Three changes: (1) Replaced single pattern regex with arrays of 7 event-driven and 5 direct-call signal regexes — a session matches if ANY signal matches, not just one specific regex. (2) Dynamic session selection: iterate ALL sessions after session 1, skip those that don't touch notification-related code (notification|adapter|service.*notify|event.*bus), rather than hardcoded indices. (3) Extracted checkConsistencyAgainstPattern as a private helper method for reuse from multiple code paths. Session 1 pattern detection also broadened — uses signal count comparison (eventScore vs directScore) instead of single boolean regex.

alternatives.option: Add more regexes to the single pattern regex (OR them together) without changing session selection
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would fix the false-negative problem for sessions 2 and 6 but would still check hardcoded sessions that may not touch notification code, and miss sessions that do. Dynamic selection is more robust to prompt/agent variation.

alternatives.option: Use LLM judge for pattern consistency evaluation
alternatives.reason_rejected: Per decision 01KN8YSGRSVY8E19P02PWZYSQ8, automated scorer should discriminate with richer sub-dimensions before adding LLM cost. The regex approach works if the signal set is broad enough.

alternatives.option: Weight sessions by signal count (partial credit per session)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adds complexity without clear benefit. The core problem was false negatives from narrow detection, not insufficient granularity. matchCount >= 1 is the right threshold since any single signal matching indicates the pattern was followed.

assumptions: Sessions that touch notification-related code will contain at least one of: notification, adapter, service.*notify, event.*bus in their diffs
The 7 event-driven signals and 5 direct-call signals cover the realistic vocabulary agents use
Session 1 always establishes the pattern (the first session designs the notification system)
